Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix performance issues in news and filings backfill

- Use executemany() for batch inserts in backfill_news_polygon,
  backfill_news_finnhub, and backfill_8k_for_ticker, replacing per-record
  execute() loops. News is the highest-volume dataset so this has the
  most impact.

- Add limit=1000 param to fetch_8k_filings to reduce pagination round-trips,
  mirroring the same fix applied to corporate actions endpoints.

- Eliminate redundant dict copy in _extract_sentiment: was doing
  enriched = dict(article) on every article before adding 2 keys.
  Since news.py re-extracts sentiment from insights itself, the copy was
  wasted work. Now mutates in-place instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix audit logs real-time updates and OMR scanner alignment error handling

- Replace one-time getDocs fetch with onSnapshot in AuditLogs.tsx for real-time updates
- Add subscribeLogs() method to AuditLogger service using Firestore onSnapshot
- Show alignment error on camera mode instead of results mode
- Return early when corner markers not detected to prevent misaligned bubble markers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix memory leak in corporate actions backfill

- Cache tenacity retry wrapper as self._retrying_execute in PolygonClient.__init__
  instead of recreating a new closure on every HTTP request. Hundreds of wrapper
  objects were accumulating during pagination loops, causing GC pressure and slowness.

- Add limit=1000 param to fetch_dividends, fetch_splits, fetch_short_interest to
  reduce pagination round-trips (Polygon default page size is small).

- Use executemany() for batch inserts in all three backfill_*_for_ticker functions
  instead of a per-record execute() loop.

- Wrap PolygonClient in a with context manager in run_full_backfill so the
  httpx.Client connection pool is always released on exit.

- Add regression tests: retry wrapper identity check across requests, limit param
  assertions on all three corporate actions fetch methods.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility audit — zero violations across all pages (#84)

* Initial plan

* fix: resolve WCAG 2.1 AA color-contrast violations across all public pages

- Darken --text-muted CSS variable from #9A8A80 (3.27:1) to #7A6A60 (5.09:1)
- Add bg-[var(--btn-primary)] solid fallback to all gradient buttons so
  axe-core can compute white text contrast (gradients are invisible to axe)
- Use accessible --btn-primary (#C0524A, 4.55:1) for interactive text links
  that previously used low-contrast --accent-rose / --accent-warm colors
- Fix StockBadge: replace opacity-modified backgrounds with pre-computed hex
  values (#EEF2E9/#FCECE7) and use dark accessible text (#366132/#7A2020)
- Fix about page sage ValueCard: replace bg-[var(--accent-sage)]/20 with
  pre-computed #EEF2E9 so axe resolves the background correctly
- Fix CopilotDevLoginButton: amber-600 → amber-800 for dev-only warning text
- Fix register page: text-gray-400 (optional) label → text-[var(--text-secondary)]
- Add @axe-core/playwright dependency and accessibility.spec.ts Playwright test
  covering all public and authenticated routes (WCAG 2.1 AA via axe-core)
- Add accessibility-public and accessibility-authenticated projects to
  playwright.config.ts

Co-authored-by: konarsubhojit <20913717+konarsubhojit@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix: DB::raw() expression cannot be concatenated as string

DB::raw() returns an Expression object, not a string, so it cannot
be concatenated with ' as tag_name'. Move the alias into the raw SQL
expression directly.

Add: php artisan migrate --force to deployment tasks

Ensures any pending database migrations are automatically applied
on every deployment, preventing 500 errors from missing tables.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[Pytorch] Add ignore_raw_node option to GraphPickler (#176939)

Summary:
The Graph Pickler raises "Unexpected raw Node during pickling" when it encounters a raw `torch.fx.Node` during serialization. This occurs in some backward graphs (e.g. IDXCVR model__27_backward_7) where metadata contains Node references that haven't been converted to `_NodePickleData`.

This diff adds an `ignore_raw_node` option to `GraphPickler.Options` that, when enabled, silently pickles unexpected raw Nodes as `None` instead of raising an `AssertionError`. This allows graph serialization to finish even when graphs contain these edge-case references.

Test Plan: buck test fbcode//mtia/accuracy/training/tests:test_graph_serialize; arc lint — no new issues

Reviewed By: aorenste

Differential Revision: D95510476

Pull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/176939
Approved by: https://github.com/aorenste

Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: PyTorch MergeBot <pytorchmergebot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial foundry-networking repository structure

- scenarios/scenarios.md: 12 networking test scenarios with Mermaid diagrams
  covering inbound (PE, IP filtering), outbound (managed VNet, BYO VNet,
  managed PE, hub-spoke), hybrid (VPN Gateway, App Gateway), and infra
  (DNS validation, trusted services)
- infra/main.bicep: orchestrator deploying VNet, Foundry, Storage, Cosmos DB,
  AI Search, Key Vault, Private Endpoints, DNS zones, Bastion + test VM
- infra/modules/: 9 reusable Bicep modules (vnet, foundry, storage, cosmosdb,
  ai-search, keyvault, private-endpoint, private-dns-zone, bastion,
  log-analytics)
- infra_deploy.ps1: main deployment script with provider registration, RG
  creation, Bicep deployment, RBAC, and VM access configuration
- lab_start.ps1 / lab_stop.ps1: start/stop lab resources to manage costs
- foundry_info.ps1: display lab resource status and networking config
- get_logs.ps1: retrieve diagnostic logs from Log Analytics

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: redesign logging system to eliminate session log flood in production

The old URLs currently redirect, but will eventually stop working.

* ✅ 添加 Playwright E2E 测试及 GM API 功能测试 (#1283)

* ✅ 添加 Playwright E2E 测试

- 配置 Playwright 使用 --headless=new 模式加载扩展
- 在 CI workflow 中添加 E2E 测试 job

* ✅ 添加 GM API E2E 测试

新增 gm-api.spec.ts 测试三类 GM API：
- GM_ 同步 API (gm_api_test.js): 29 项测试
- GM.* 异步 API (gm_api_async_test.js): 29 项测试
- Content 注入测试 (inject_content_test.js): 11 项测试

实现要点：
- 两阶段浏览器启动：Phase 1 启用 userScriptsAccess，Phase 2 重启运行测试

- 去除 @require/@resource 的 SRI hash 避免校验失败

更新 utils.ts 中 installScriptByCode 增加保存失败的 fallback 检测

* 🐛 修复 GM API E2E 测试 CI 兼容性

- Phase 1 添加 --headless=new 参数，修复 CI 无 X server 环境
- 添加 eslint-disable 注释消除 Playwright use() 的误报
- prettier 格式化修正

* 🐛 修复 E2E 测试 CI 兼容性问题

- vitest.config.ts: 排除 e2e/ 目录避免 Vitest 误跑 Playwright 测试
- eslint.config.mjs: 为 e2e/ 目录关闭 react-hooks/rules-of-hooks 规则
- e2e/options.spec.ts: 菜单正则加 /i 标志修复英文环境大小写匹配
- prettier 格式化修正

* 🚑 修复其他扩展注入 chrome.runtime 导致环境误判的问题 #1280 (#1281)

* 🐛 修复其他扩展注入 chrome.runtime 导致环境误判的问题 #1280

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: pre-compute quoted column names for DataTable.Update RowUpdating handler

The RowUpdating event fires once per INSERT row during DataTable.Update().
Previously the handler iterated all columns, called commandBuilder.QuoteIdentifier
for every refreshable column, and allocated a ResizeArray on every row.

Column metadata (AutoIncrement/AllowDBNull flags) and quoted column names are
constant for the lifetime of a single Update() call, so we pre-compute them
once before subscribing:

- alwaysRefreshQuoted: quoted names for AutoIncrement columns (server always
  supplies the value)
- maybeRefreshCols: (DataColumn, quotedName) pairs for AllowDBNull columns;
  only added per-row when the row has no explicit value

This eliminates O(columns) QuoteIdentifier calls and a full column-iteration
per INSERT row, replacing them with a single pre-computation and a cheaper
per-row scan over the smaller maybeRefreshCols array.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- New pkg/swagger/ shared package — RegisterRoutes(router, title, spec)
  serves Swagger UI at /swagger/ for any service
- All import paths updated from github.com/showbiz-io/... to
  github.com/shaharby7/...

Swagger UI added to both services:
- API: /swagger/ serves full OpenAPI spec with all 38 endpoints
- FakeProvider: /swagger/ serves machine CRUD spec
- Each service embeds its own openapi.yaml via //go:embed

Dockerfile updated:
- Build context is now repo root (copies go.mod, pkg/, service dir)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

arch: ADR-024 single Go module with shared pkg/ directory

Document that the entire repo uses a single Go module
(github.com/shaharby7/showbiz) with a top-level pkg/ directory for
shared packages. Services import shared code from pkg/ and keep
service-specific code in their own internal/.

- architecture/decisions.md: add ADR-024
- architecture/development.md: add Go Module Structure section, update
  directory tree to show go.mod at root, pkg/, and docs/

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: add Swagger UI instruction for backend services

All backend services must expose a Swagger UI at /swagger/ with an
OpenAPI spec kept in sync with the code.

chore: add GitHub Copilot instructions

2. Docs after implementation — update docs/ to reflect what was actually built

not from Supabase Storage. No environment variables required.

- Remove mandatory NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_FIGURES_URL requirement

- Update local dev instructions (no API route or env vars needed)
- Update diagnostic section (check web/public/figures/ not Supabase URLs)

fix: serve figures as static web assets (web/public/figures/)

- Add scripts/web/copy_figures_to_web.py: copies needed figures from
  figures/ (gitignored scientific output) to web/public/figures/
  (committed Next.js static assets). Run when figures are regenerated.

- Update web/src/lib/utils.ts: figureUrl() now returns /figures/<path>
  pointing to web/public/ instead of /api/figures (filesystem) route.
  Supabase URL override still available via NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_FIGURES_URL.

- Update scripts/web/extract_composite_site_data.py: checks existence in
  web/public/figures/ep_structure/ (where files are now committed).

- Copy 36 figures to web/public/figures/ (cluster, main, ep_structure).
  composite_figures_manifest.json now shows exists: true for all 15 entries.

 /api/figures
returned 404. Fix: serve from web/public/ which is committed and deployed.
